About: It is about two British policemen kissing.
Affects: It affects the perception of authority and law enforcement.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Banksy's notoriety as a provocative street artist and activist.
It is associated with Michelangelo's Sistine Ceiling.
It is associated with the LGBTQ+ Capital of the UK.
It is associated with the LGBTQ community in Brighton.
It is associated with the country's attitude toward homosexuality.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the genre of stencil graffiti.
CausedBy: It is caused by Banksy's artistic expression.
ConsistsOf: It consists of two police officers painted in black and white.
It consists of evident handcuffs and a baton around their respective belts.
CreatedIn: It was created in Brighton, England.
CreatedTo: It was created to deliver a critique of an aspect of society.
It was created to orient public attention and conversation to their intended political or social message.
It was created to normalize and cultivate an increased societal acceptance of homosexuality.
HasA: It has a portrayal of same-sex intimacy.
It has iconic figures and images deconstructed and juxtaposed into an atypical context.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its strategic placement.
It is characterized by the speed and ease of stencil graffiti.
It is characterized by the deconstruction and juxtaposition of iconic figures and images into an atypical context.
It is characterized by considerable damage due to repeated vandalism.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Michelangelo's Sistine Ceiling.
It was influenced by Banksy's own remarks on street art.
It was influenced by the city's rich history with the LGBTQ community.
IsA: It is a provocative work of street art.
It is a cultural staple of Brighton.
It is a revolutionary piece of art.
KnownAs: It is known as Kissing Coppers.
KnownFor: It is known for being one of Banksy’s most notable works.
It is known for its portrayal of two British policemen kissing.
It is known for its strategic placement.
It is known for its deconstruction and juxtaposition of iconic figures and images.
LocatedIn: It is located on the wall of The Prince Albert pub in Brighton.
It is located in Brighton, England.
It is located in London for The Other Art Fair.
OccurredAt: It occurred at The Prince Albert pub in Brighton.
It occurred at an auction in Miami.
PartOf: It is part of Banksy's body of work.
It is part of the history of street art.
It is part of the cultural landscape of Brighton.
PublishedAs: It was published as a stencil graffiti.
UsedAs: It is used as an example of stencil graffiti.
It is used as a critique of society.
It is used as a symbol of Brighton's LGBTQ+ community.
UsedFor: It is used for orienting public attention and conversation.
It is used for delivering a critique of an aspect of society.
It is used for normalizing and cultivating increased societal acceptance of homosexuality.
UsedIn: It is used in street art.
It is used in the context of political and social messages.
It is used in Brighton's cultural identity.
UsedTo: It is used to keep Banksy's anonymity.
It is used to orient public attention and conversation.
It is used to deliver a critique of an aspect of society.
